title: soliva sessilis
intro: Soliva sessilis, also known as the creeping marshwort or creeping soliva, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to tropical and subtropical regions. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help soothe wounds and reduce pain. The health benefits of Soliva sessilis include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antimicrobial agent to combat infections. Herbal preparations of Soliva sessilis can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
